text: [Music]
now earthquake has triggered a tsunami
which has struck a coastal city in
Indonesia
witnesses say the giant wave swept into
the streets of palü which is on the
island of Sulawesi his rebecca henskee
in the Indonesian capital of Jakarta
mobile phone footage shows the
terrifying moment that up to 3 meter
high waves hit the coastal area in the
city of Paulo the footage shows the
water sweeping into houses closest to
the shore other footage taken on mobile
phones from ships out to sea show the
huge waves hitting the port area with
people praying and screaming you can
hear in the footage officials have
confirmed that a tsunami has hit the
area but say casualty figures are still
unclear telecommunications to the region
has been cut off they're sending in
rescue teams as we speak to reach the
area and to get a clearer picture of
what's taken place here the quake
happened around 5 p.m. local time and
was 7.7 magnitude a significant quake
for Indonesia living in the Ring of Fire
a country that's no stranger to
earthquakes after suffering heavy quakes
in Lombok in July and August that killed
over 500 people what's happened today in
Paulo is still to become clear Rebekah
henskee BBC News Jakarta
